items.title: Children become victims of unprecedented violence in Chile
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

Agustina Pascale became one of the first public faces of an unprecedented wave of killings that is on track to leave a record number of minors as victims of criminal gangs this year—whether recruited or killed in the crossfire. By mid-November, a total of 48 children had been killed by firearms, six more than were recorded in all of 2023, officials said.

items.title: Meet the camels that take part in Spain's festive Three Kings tradition
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
In many Spanish families, children receive presents from the Three Kings on Epiphany, Jan. 6, rather than from Santa on Christmas Day. The night before, towns across Spain celebrate with parades re-enacting the Magi’s journey to Bethlehem, when actors portraying the wise men are often accompanied by camels. The animals are a much-loved part of the tradition, and children often leave carrots out for them in their homes, just as they do for Santa's reindeer. But if camels make an appearance on Spanish streets just one day a year, where are they the rest of the time?
